figures.caption: Fig. 3. An illustration of the diverse medical imaging tasks addressed by our model, spanning lesion classification (dermoscopic images), organ classification (CT scans), blood cell classification (microscopy), tissue classification (histopathology), and disease detection (CT & X-ray scans), with representative example classes from each dataset.
TABLE 5 Performance comparison across five benchmark datasets using a reversed task order. The best and second-best results are bolded and underlined, respectively.

Fig. 4. The performance curves across learning sessions on five medical datasets. Bi-CRCL consistently achieves the best overall performance and exhibits minimal degradation as class number increases.
TABLE 3 Performance comparison using different PFMs (medical-specific vs. general-domain) across five benchmark datasets. The best results of each PFM are bolded.
